education.degree: Master of Software Engineering in Scalable Systems
education.description: Graduate study in scalable systems design and software engineering.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science and Statistics, Minor in Mathematics, Honors
education.description: Dual degree in Computer Science and Statistics with a Mathematics minor.

bullets: Engineered infrastructure for rapid recovery of services to reduce critical downtime on the Infrastructure/Continuous Deployment team
Saved dozens of engineer hours by enabling bulk service redeployment for incident managers without engineer intervention
Led development of a tool capable of redistributing 10,000+ service instances across ~15 hardware cells during setup and failover
Implemented fault-tolerant Temporal workflows in Go capable of triggering thousands of Nomad jobs in batches
Built infra for company-wide big data visualization using Apache Superset on the Foundation AI/Data Infrastructure team
Designed systems leveraging AWS ELB, EKS, ElastiCache/Redis, RDS, etc. to handle 400k+ daily requests and database queries
Minimized manual overhead by automating cluster deployment using Terraform CDK in Go for provisioning and GHA for CI/CD
Coordinated with PMs to migrate 2500+ dashboards for 900 users to Superset 4.0.1 while minimizing critical dashboard downtime
Researched improvements on mine detection and classification from drone imagery for the MU Information and Data Fusion Lab
Increased PyTorch model training speed by 16x by engineering SLURM pipelines with dynamic resource allocation on an HPC cluster
Improved mine detection by augmenting 250,000+ real/simulated mine images resulting in a 10% increase in AUC
Streamlined the previously manual and error-prone generation of 3D maps of US cities from LiDAR data using the ArcGIS Python SDK
Proposed improvement to building and vegetation classification by training a PointNet model on 700,000+ 3D LiDAR points
Reduced generation time of 3.6 million NYC building footprint labels by 800% using Apache Spark for parallelized computations

projects.name: King's Eye
projects.description: A mobile app that converts chess board images into digital, interactive boards. Uses YOLOv8 classification hosted on AWS Lambda for board recognition.

projects.name: Algorithmic Trading Platform
projects.description: A web app for creating, testing, and monitoring hundreds of trading strategies in real-time with Kubernetes-backed low-latency live trading.

projects.description: RAG data augmenter for generating synthetic training data using Chroma DB and OpenAI/Groq.

extracurriculars.description: Led the growth to 40+ active members and 250+ followers, managed the $6000 budget, and established SASE as a permanent organization within the College of Engineering
Guided new students through college and the professional world as the leader of the Intro to CS Special Interest Group
